steps.title: Identify the list structure
steps.content: The sentence introduces a reference point on the left (“vast distances from Earth”) and provides a series of specific examples on the right (the exoplanets with their distances). The blank must properly connect these two elements.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
Option A places a colon after “Earth” to introduce the series of exoplanet examples that follows. A colon introduces a list or elaboration, which is the appropriate rule for this structure.

Options B, C, and D either misplace the colon, create a confusing appositive phrase, or omit necessary punctuation.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
